channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: April 15 is Jackie Robinson Day around Major League Baseball
item.description: Jackie Robinson’s legacy is being celebrated around the major leagues, with the day named for the first Black baseball player of the modern era and marking the 78th anniversary of his debut for the Brooklyn Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dodger Stadium to host baseball, with surfing and cricket outside Los Angeles at 2028 Olympics
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The 2028 Los Angeles Olympics will feature baseball at Dodger Stadium in its return to the program with surfing and cricket held outside the city as part of an updated venue plan released Tuesday by the organizing committee.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rome derby clashes likened to 'urban warfare' with 24 police officers injured
item.description: ROME (AP) — The toll from clashes between violent fans and police at the weekend's Rome derby includes 24 law enforcement officers injured, seven arrests and at least 40,000 euros ($45,000) in destroyed garbage dumpsters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Warriors host Memphis Grizzlies in NBA play-in game Tuesday at Chase Center
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (BCN) -- The Golden State Warriors missed a chance to clinch a National Basketball Association playoff spot in their last regular season game on Sunday but will get another chance when they host a play-in game Tuesday against the Memphis Grizzlies at Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Tyron Smith set to retire with Cowboys after final season with Jets, AP source says
item.description: Tyron Smith is set to retire with the Dallas Cowboys after the perennial Pro Bowl left tackle spent his final season with the New York Jets, a person with knowledge of Smith's plans said Tuesday. The Cowboys set a Wednesday announcement from Smith without providing any details.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: World Cup poster for New York/New Jersey revealed
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The official 2026 World Cup poster for New York/New Jersey has been revealed. The New York New Jersey Host Committee unveiled the poster on Monday. It was designed by Rich Tu, a Brooklyn-based artist from New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: The NBA playoffs will, once again, be an international showcase of stars
item.description: They’ll be watching in Canada, not just because of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, though the NBA’s scoring champion and MVP favorite who plays for Oklahoma City surely helps lure in fans who are north of the border.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rory McIlroy has the Masters and a Grand Slam. That gives him freedom to chase more
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The Mount Rushmore of golf now has six faces carved into granite, and for that Rory McIlroy should feel as though he achieved the highest pinnacle in golf. But there is so much more ahead for McIlroy.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Celebrities and athletes you didn't know appeared at WrestleMania
item.description: President Donald Trump, Regis Philbin, Alice Cooper and several others made appearances at WrestleMania over the years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PWHL picks Ottawa to host 3rd draft in June, with Wisconsin's Casey O'Brien among top prospects
item.description: The PWHL will hold its third annual entry draft in Ottawa on June 24, the league announced on Tuesday, with University of Wisconsin’s Casey O’Brien anticipated to be one of the top selections following her MVP senior season.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WNBA draftees turn attention to making rosters as training camp opens in a few weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Paige Bueckers will be heading to Dallas, the latest transcendent college star to go No. 1 in the WNBA draft. Her spot is assured with the Wings.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cody Rhodes to launch podcast as part of WWE's partnership with Fanatics
item.description: Cody Rhodes will launch the "What Do You Wanna Talk About" podcast as part of WWE's expanded partnership with Fanatics, the company announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roster limit changes not offered in latest filing on $2.8 billion NCAA lawsuit settlement
item.description: Attorneys working on the $2.8 billion legal settlement designed to reshape college sports filed a brief Monday night that did not include changes a judge suggested regarding team roster limits, saying such a late change to that rule would create havoc.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Keller's four points leads Utah past Nashville, 7-3
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (ABC4 Sports) - With nothing to play for but pride, the Utah Hockey Club is out to finish the season strong.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado will retire Shedeur Sanders’ No. 2 and Travis Hunter's No. 12 in ceremony at spring game
item.description: Shedeur Sanders and Travis Hunter left big footprints in only two seasons on the field at Colorado. For that, their numbers will be retired Saturday at the spring game as part of the afternoon's festivities. Sanders’ No. 2 and Hunter’s No.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cam Ward leads group of former hidden gems turned NFL prospects
item.description: Back then, Ward worked behind center in his high school's run-heavy Wing-T offense. His roughly 12 throws per game didn't give him much chance to match the gaudy numbers of top recruits, and not many college coaches were making the one-hour drive from Houston to his hometown of roughly 4,000 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aaron Judge salutes 'brave men and women' fighting for country after being named Team USA captain for WBC
item.description: Aaron Judge was named Team USA's captain for the 2026 World Baseball Classic, and during his press conference, he saluted the "brave men and women who have fought for our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz reflect on tough season, look to the future
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz finished the 2024-25 season not with just the worst record in the NBA, but the worst in franchise history. At 17-65, Jazz fans are extremely frustrated, but the Jazz brass insist brighter days are ahead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Crochet is still Crochet, even if Red now precedes 'Sox' on the front of his jersey
item.description: Garrett Crochet, the former White Sox pitcher, made his debut for the Boston Red Sox on Sunday, shutting down the Chicago White Sox with 11 strikeouts and one run. Despite the trade, Crochet has expressed his fondness for his former team and the memories he made on Chicago's South Side.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks score 4 3rd-period goals for 5-2 win over Bruins
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Nick Foligno scored a pair of goals, Arvid Soderblom made 31 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Boston Bruins 5-2 on Thursday night. Ryan Donato, Tyler Bertuzzi and Kevin Korchinski also had goals for the Blackhawks, who scored four goals in third period to pull away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls beat Heat behind Giddey's triple-double, move ahead of Miami for play-in home game
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Bulls have just one game left at the United Center on their regular-season schedule. But they took a big step toward securing at least one more home game for the Eastern Conference play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Cavaliers claim the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Bulls
item.description: Darius Garland scored 28 points, Evan Mobley had 21 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists and the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Chicago Bulls on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
